plan: Identify the effusion regime and the relevant velocity distribution for escaping molecules

Set up the infinitesimal Carnot cycle using T and P as state variables, identifying the four quasi-static steps between (T, P) and (T+dT, P+dP)
Express entropy S as a function of T and P by using the Maxwell relation derived from the thermodynamic identity, relating partial derivatives of S and E
Use the Curie law relation to find chi(T) and express E in terms of P and T, then compute dE/dT at constant P
Apply the Carnot cycle efficiency condition (net work = net heat) to derive a differential equation relating f(P) to the Maxwell relation result
Integrate the constraint to find f(P) and write the full expression for U(T, P), then simplify using Curie law to express in terms of C and T only

Set up the ray geometry: define the Moon's elevation angle (3°) and model the observer as at infinity (parallel ray approximation), treating the ocean surface as a flat mirror tilted by small ripple angles.

Model ocean ripple facets as a mirror tilted by a small angle δ in an arbitrary azimuthal direction φ, and apply the law of reflection to find how the reflected ray direction shifts as a function of (δ, φ).

Project the shifted reflected ray onto the observer's image plane to find the apparent displacement of the reflection image in the vertical (elevation) and horizontal (azimuth) directions as functions of δ and φ.

Find the maximum vertical displacement by optimizing over φ for fixed δ (occurs at φ=0, i.e., ripple tilt in the vertical plane), and find the maximum horizontal displacement (occurs at φ=90°, ripple tilt in horizontal plane), expressing both maxima in terms of δ and the elevation angle θ=3°.
Compute the ratio r = max_vertical / max_horizontal, simplify using θ=3°, and evaluate numerically, then round to the nearest whole number.
Express the original disk magnet field at center as B0, noting it depends on R, d, and magnetization M via the thin-disk limit formula for on-axis field.
Apply superposition: treat the annular magnet as the full disk (radius 2R) minus a smaller disk (radius R), both with the same magnetization M and thickness d.
Use the thin-disk on-axis center field formula to find the field at the center of the small disk of radius R with the same M and d.
Express B_small(R) in terms of B0 by taking the ratio B_small/B_full using their functional dependence on radius, then solve for B1 = B_full - B_small = B0 - B_small.

Plan controls and validation experiments: prepare single-color compensation beads or cells for each fluorochrome, FMO (fluorescence-minus-one) controls for all transcription factors and key surface markers, unstained and isotype controls, and validate panel on known positive/negative cell lines or sorted populations before running experimental samples

Establish gating hierarchy and data analysis pipeline: gate singlets → live cells → lineage-negative (dump−) → CD56+CD127± ILC/NK gate → subset into ILC1 (T-bet+Eomes−), ILC2 (GATA3+CRTH2+), ILC3 (RORγt+NKp44±), CD56bright NK (Eomes+T-bet+CD94hi), CD56dim NK (Eomes+T-bet+CD16+), and adaptive NK (NKG2C+FcεRIγ−); quantify transcription factor MFI per subset

Model the crossing scheme and define m_k: F0 has genotype aA^(n-1) with m_0=1. F1 is a self-cross of F0 giving m_1=2. For k>=2, generation k = F_{k-1} x F_{k-2}, and m_k is the maximum number of 'a' alleles achievable in any individual of generation k.

Derive the capped recurrence m_k = min(m_{k-1}, h) + min(m_{k-2}, h) where h = n/2. Each parent of ploidy n contributes a gamete of size n/2, so the maximum 'a' alleles in a gamete from a parent with k 'a' alleles is min(k, h). Summing the best gametes from each parent gives the recurrence.
Compute the uncapped Fibonacci sequence (valid while all m_k < h): m_0=1, m_1=2, m_2=3, m_3=5, m_4=8, m_5=13, m_6=21. These are the actual values of the capped recurrence for any h > 21.

Derive conditions on h for the all-'a' genotype to first appear at generation 8. Require m_8=2h (appears at 8) but m_7<2h (not at 7). m_8=2h needs m_7>=h AND m_6>=h. m_7<2h with m_6>=h means m_7 = h + min(m_5,h) < 2h, so min(m_5,h) < h, i.e., m_5 < h. Combined: m_5 < h <= m_6 (using capped values), which with the Fibonacci reference gives 13 < h <= 21.

Verify minimum n=28 (h=14): m_5=13<14, m_6=21>=14, m_7=min(21,14)+min(13,14)=14+13=27<28, m_8=min(27,14)+min(21,14)=14+14=28=n. The all-'a' genotype first appears exactly in generation 8. Check n=26 (h=13): m_5=13=h violates m_5<h so all-'a' appears at generation 7, confirming n=28 is the minimum.
